title: Coding with AI
content: Artificial Intelligence is rapidly becoming one of the most valuable tools available to modern software developers. At BarkinMad Studios, AI is used as a collaborative development assistant to help accelerate coding, debugging, user interface design, localisation, documentation, content creation, and rapid prototyping across multiple projects.

As an independent developer managing several applications and games, efficiency is important. AI tools help reduce the time spent on repetitive tasks, allowing more focus on design, gameplay, testing, user experience, and long-term product improvements.

Projects such as [ZXSnake](/apps/zxsnake), [ZXBrick](/apps/zxbrick), [ZXSpace](/apps/zxspace), [ZXPong](/apps/zxpong), and the wider [ZX Series](/zx-series) have all benefited from AI-assisted workflows. AI can explain unfamiliar concepts, suggest alternative approaches, identify potential bugs, generate example code, and help troubleshoot development challenges—allowing developers to solve problems more quickly while still applying their own judgement.

Beyond coding, AI also assists with content creation. Website articles, development logs, marketing content, localisation reviews, documentation, release notes, and support materials can all be created more efficiently with AI acting as a collaborative assistant. This allows a small independent studio to maintain a larger online presence without sacrificing development time.

One area where AI has proven particularly useful is rapid prototyping. New ideas can be explored quickly by generating proof-of-concept code, testing different user interface approaches, and evaluating technical solutions before significant development effort is invested. This helps reduce risk and speeds up the process of turning ideas into working software.

AI is also valuable when learning new technologies. Modern software development evolves quickly, with new frameworks, APIs, services, and tools appearing regularly. AI can help explain documentation, provide examples, compare approaches, and accelerate the learning process. This allows developers to adopt new technologies more efficiently while still understanding how the underlying systems work.

However, AI is not a replacement for experienced software developers. Successful software products still require human creativity, experience, planning, and judgement. Decisions around architecture, product direction, gameplay design, performance optimisation, security, accessibility, testing, and user experience remain the responsibility of the developer.

At BarkinMad Studios, AI is viewed as a productivity tool rather than an autonomous developer. All generated code is reviewed, tested, and adapted to fit the requirements of each project. Human oversight remains essential to ensure software quality, maintainability, and long-term reliability.

The rise of AI has also changed how independent developers can compete. Tasks that once required large teams can now be completed by smaller teams or even individual developers. While AI does not remove the need for expertise, it allows experienced developers to spend more time solving meaningful problems and less time performing repetitive work.

As AI technology continues to evolve, BarkinMad Studios will continue exploring responsible ways to incorporate these tools into everyday development workflows. The goal is not to replace creativity, but to enhance it by removing barriers, accelerating development, and allowing more time to focus on building useful applications and enjoyable games.

For independent studios, Used responsibly, AI allows independent developers to work smarter, experiment faster, and deliver higher-quality software without compromising creativity or craftsmanship. At BarkinMad Studios, AI is another tool—one that complements experience, curiosity, and careful engineering rather than replacing them.
